metadata.title: SSI Payment Schedule 2026 | Check SSI Payment Dates
metadata.description: Estimate SSI payment dates for 2026 and 2027. Learn why SSI can arrive before the 1st when the date falls on a weekend or federal holiday.

hero.title: SSI Payment Schedule
hero.description: SSI is usually scheduled for the 1st of the month. When the 1st falls on a weekend or federal holiday, the payment is usually scheduled earlier.
hero.tip: Independent tool. Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Social Security Administration.

checker.title: Check an SSI payment date
checker.description: Select SSI only or Social Security + SSI to estimate scheduled dates for a month.

rules.title: Why SSI sometimes comes early
rules.description: SSI follows a simple monthly rule, but weekends and federal holidays can move the scheduled date earlier.

items.title: Usually the 1st
items.description: SSI is generally scheduled on the 1st day of the month.

items.title: Weekend adjustment
items.description: If the 1st is a Saturday or Sunday, the scheduled date usually moves to the previous Friday.

items.title: Holiday adjustment
items.description: If the 1st is a federal holiday, the scheduled date can move to the previous business day.

items.title: Not benefit management
items.description: This site cannot update direct deposit, Direct Express, or benefit records.

official_sources.title: Official sources for SSI payment dates
official_sources.description: Use the checker for quick planning, then use SSA and Treasury pages for official calendar publications, benefit records, and payment-method changes.
official_sources.tip: Social Security Dates is independent and does not collect sensitive personal or banking information.

items.title: SSA payment calendar
items.description: Official SSA page for Social Security and SSI payment schedules and calendar publications.

items.title: SSA 2026 schedule PDF
items.description: SSA Publication No. 05-10031 for 2026 scheduled benefit payment dates.

items.title: SSA missing payment FAQ
items.description: Official guidance for late, missing, or stolen Social Security payments.

items.title: Go Direct
items.description: U.S. Treasury resource for electronic federal benefit payment information.

items.question: Why are there sometimes two SSI payments in one month?
items.answer: When the next month's 1st falls on a weekend or holiday, that payment may be scheduled at the end of the prior month. It is usually an early payment for the next month, not an extra benefit.

items.question: What if I receive SSI and Social Security?
items.answer: The checker can show both estimated dates. SSI usually follows the 1st rule, while Social Security often follows the 3rd rule for people receiving both.

items.question: Where should I change direct deposit?
items.answer: Use official SSA.gov or GoDirect.gov channels. This tool does not collect bank information.

cta.title: Estimate your SSI date
cta.description: Use the checker without sharing sensitive personal or banking information.
